candidates.stormEvent: Flash Flood impact in Broward County
candidates.narrative:
Social media depicts several stalled cars along NW 4th Street in Deerfield Beach. Water level is about halfway up SUV size tires.

Flash flooding reported on Sunrise Blvd and NE 3rd Ave in Fort Lauderdale.
candidates.scoreReasoning: Flash Flood carries a 18-point severity weight. Event is within the last 180 days (+4). NOAA reported impacts across multiple county sub-areas (+8). Reported property damage exceeded $10K (+4). Narrative indicates water intrusion or flooding (+5). Narrative suggests broad impact across the service area (+4).

candidates.stormEvent: Thunderstorm Wind impact in Broward County

An off duty NWS employee reported several large tree branches down near the intersection of NW 99th Ave and Johnson Street as a severe thunderstorm downburst occurred.

candidates.stormEvent: Wildfire impact in Broward County
candidates.narrative: Lightning from a thunderstorm sparked the Mile Marker 39 fire in western Broward County on the afternoon of August 18, 2025. The fire consumed 48,000 acres before it was fully contained on August 26, 2025.
candidates.scoreReasoning: Wildfire carries a 18-point severity weight.

candidates.stormEvent: Tornado impact in Palm Beach County
candidates.narrative: A supercell thunderstorm moved northeast across Palm Beach County, generally from Lion Country Safari, through Westlake, and into the Acreage. This storm did not produce a continuous tornado, however, brief EF0 level touchdowns were identified in the damage survey. In general, the majority of the damage was located in the Acreage and was confined to minor tree damage at the low end of the EF0 scale, and damage to outdoor equipment and furniture. There were, however, a couple locations where large pines were snapped near the trunk, resulting in a high end EF0 classification.
candidates.scoreReasoning: Tornado carries a 18-point severity weight.
